title: Why OSU (4/19/21 — sustainability + climate-as-generation-fight)
html:
TL;DR

  • Three-paragraph fragment answering a transfer/scholarship "Why OSU?" prompt — never finished.
  • Yahir at ~18 frames Oregon State University as the business-meets-sustainability play: "pursue business in a manner that would produce a net positive effect for the earth."
  • Names climate change as "the fight of my generation" — earliest dated mention in the corpus.
  • Pairs business ambition with triple bottom line thinking; school chosen partly for "the influence of nature."
  • Sibling to the April 9 scholarship grind — same week, same scholarship-essay mode.

Notes

A scaffold, not a finished essay. Three sentences in, Yahir trails off mid-thought ("OSU looked like a school that" — sentence never closes). Useful primarily as a datestamp on the climate-as-generation-fight framing before that frame got absorbed into broader entrepreneurship registers.

The "business with net positive earth-effect" framing here predates Yahir's eventual pivot from business to CS (documented in the March 2022 OSIG pivot doc) — and predates the Brotein / Rankett product cluster entirely. This is pre-builder Yahir still framing impact through the climate lens, before ambition routed through software.

Note the contrast with later docs: by the 2023 OSIG retrospective he's no longer thinking in climate-fight terms — the crypto / crypto club thesis has displaced sustainability as the operative ambition vector. Worth flagging for the career vector synthesis.

Connections

  • osu · college admissions · scholarship grind 2021
  • climate change · sustainability triple bottom line
  • 2021 04 09 rudy sanchez scolarships · 2021 01 12 fhs final · 2022 04 12 instead of osig
